notes: Public adverts avoid client names, tenant names, and exact project identifiers.
Content below uses verified employer context plus role-safe industry-standard wording.

factsUsed: Project management and commercial advisory services for large-scale data centre and infrastructure developments
Support across feasibility, design, construction, commissioning, and operations
Acts on behalf of owners / developers and interfaces with contractors and specialist vendors
Trusted by data centre developers, investors, and operators across Europe, APAC, and AMERS
Supports hyperscale, colocation, and AI-enabled facility developments through commissioning and operations
Specialises in installation of HV and LV cables, data, and fiber-optic systems
Works across UK and Europe in data centre and infrastructure-related sectors
Positions itself as an electrical and data fibre installation company
Nature of business listed as electrical installation

jobs.title: Electrical Commissioning Manager — Data Centre — Frankfurt
jobs.overview: Electrical Commissioning Manager required for a client-side data centre programme in Frankfurt. The role focuses on electrical commissioning delivery across L1-L5 activities, coordinating stakeholders through pre-energisation, testing, systems interface, documentation close-out, and handover. This position suits someone confident leading commissioning workflows in mission-critical environments, maintaining strong safety, quality, and reporting standards while driving programme milestones and turnover readiness.
responsibilities: Lead electrical commissioning activities across L1-L5 delivery stages on a client-side programme.
Coordinate contractors, vendors, and delivery teams through inspections, testing, issue resolution, and turnover.
Manage commissioning trackers, witness points, progress reporting, and close-out actions.
Review documentation, test packs, red-lines, and handover records for completeness and quality.
Support interface management between construction, commissioning, and operational stakeholders.
Promote safe systems of work and consistent quality standards through energisation and handover.
requirements: Strong experience in electrical commissioning within data centre or mission-critical environments.
Working knowledge of structured L1-L5 commissioning activities and close-out processes.
Confident coordinating multiple stakeholders in a client-side or owner’s representative setting.
Able to manage commissioning documentation, issue tracking, and reporting.
Strong communication skills with a practical, delivery-focused approach.

jobs.title: Electrical Commissioning Manager — Data Centre — Paris
jobs.overview: Electrical Commissioning Manager required for a client-side data centre programme in Paris. The role centres on electrical commissioning delivery across L1-L5 activities, helping drive testing, stakeholder coordination, documentation close-out, and handover readiness. It will suit a commissioning professional who can maintain momentum across multiple interfaces while keeping safety, quality, reporting, and close-out standards under control in a mission-critical setting.
responsibilities: Lead electrical commissioning delivery across L1-L5 activities on a live client-side programme.
Coordinate contractors, specialist vendors, and delivery teams through testing and close-out phases.
Monitor commissioning progress, issue resolution, and turnover actions against project milestones.
Review test documentation, commissioning records, and handover packs for completeness and accuracy.
Support clear interface management between construction, commissioning, and operational teams.
Maintain strong safety and quality standards throughout energisation, testing, and final handover.
requirements: Strong experience in electrical commissioning on data centre or mission-critical projects.
Practical understanding of structured L1-L5 commissioning activities.
Able to manage documentation, issue tracking, and progress reporting in a live project environment.
Comfortable working across multiple stakeholder groups in a client-side setting.
Clear communication skills and a delivery-led approach to commissioning close-out.

jobs.title: Quantity Surveyor — Data Centre Cabling
jobs.overview: Quantity Surveyor required to support commercial oversight across data centre cabling projects for a specialist contractor working across HV, LV, data, and fibre installation scopes. The role will suit someone comfortable managing commercial processes across technically led electrical packages, with a focus on cost control, subcontract administration, valuations, variations, and project reporting. This is a strong fit for a QS who understands delivery risk, package change, and the commercial detail needed to support specialist installation teams.
responsibilities: Support commercial management across data centre cabling and electrical installation packages.
Prepare and review valuations, variations, cost reports, and payment applications.
Track subcontractor accounts, change control, and project commercial performance.
Work closely with operations and delivery teams on procurement, scope alignment, and package forecasting.
Maintain accurate commercial records and support final account close-out.
Provide clear reporting on cost movement, risk, and opportunity across live projects.
requirements: Quantity Surveying experience within electrical, MEP, cabling, or infrastructure packages.
Strong commercial administration, reporting, and change control capability.
Comfortable working across specialist installation scopes in live project environments.
Good stakeholder communication with operational and commercial teams.
Organised approach to records, valuations, and forecast accuracy.

jobs.title: Electrical Testing Engineer — Data Centre
jobs.overview: Electrical Testing Engineer required to support electrical inspection and testing activities on mission-critical data centre programmes. This permanent opportunity includes accommodation and travel support and will suit someone able to coordinate electricians and engineers on site while maintaining safe, organised, and high-quality testing delivery. The role combines hands-on technical oversight with team coordination, reporting, and close-out support across fast-paced critical infrastructure environments.
responsibilities: Oversee electrical inspection and testing activities across live mission-critical projects.
Coordinate site electricians and engineers to ensure testing works are planned and executed safely.
Review progress, testing records, and close-out actions to support timely completion.
Maintain clear communication with site management and wider delivery stakeholders.
Support quality, compliance, and structured handover of tested systems.
Promote strong safety standards and orderly workface coordination across testing phases.
requirements: Electrical testing or inspection experience on complex commercial or industrial projects.
Confident leading or coordinating site teams during live testing activities.
Strong understanding of safe electrical work practices and documentation close-out.
Able to communicate clearly with site teams and project stakeholders.
Mission-critical or data centre experience is advantageous.
